term: ajjhattaṁ vā kāye kāyānupassī viharati, bahiddhā vā kāye kāyānupassī viharati, ajjhattabahiddhā vā kāye kāyānupassī viharati
preferred_translation: one remains observing the body as internal, observing the body as external, and observing the body as both internal and external
alternative_translations: one remains observing the body within, observing the body outside, and observing the body both within and outside
discouraged_translations: one dwells contemplating the body internally and externally
one observes the body in oneself and in others
definition: Body-specific satipaṭṭhāna refrain line naming the internal, external, and internal-and-external contemplative turns.
notes: Use this record for the body branch refrain in MN 10. The repository keeps internal and external rather than subjective and objective, and does not recast the line into self/other commentary. The same structural pattern governs the felt-experience, feeling-mind, and dhamma branches with the expected noun substitutions.

example_phrases.pali: ajjhattaṁ vā kāye kāyānupassī viharati, bahiddhā vā kāye kāyānupassī viharati, ajjhattabahiddhā vā kāye kāyānupassī viharati
example_phrases.translation: one remains observing the body as internal, observing the body as external, and observing the body as both internal and external

authority_basis.scope: Supports the body-branch refrain pattern and its internal / external contrast.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use this as the standard English control line for the body-branch internal / external refrain unless a recorded local reason requires minor grammatical adjustment.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not psychologize the contrast into subjective versus objective, and do not recast the line into self-versus-other commentary unless a source-facing note explicitly explains why.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents the satipaṭṭhāna refrain from drifting into archaic contemplation-language or psychologized internal/external explanation.
